description_long: This bungalow features a single-story structure with a prominent, deep overhanging roof in a dark reddish-brown color. The exterior walls are clad in light wood paneling, complemented by a brick base that runs along the bottom perimeter. The front facade includes a covered porch area supported by columns, with a central entrance door flanked by large windows and additional glass panels to one side, allowing ample natural light. The side and rear elevations are simpler, with minimal window openings and a consistent use of materials. The roof is a cross-gabled style, providing architectural interest and additional shelter to the porch area. The overall design is practical and inviting, suitable for suburban or residential neighborhoods.
description: A single-story bungalow with a cross-gabled, dark reddish-brown roof, light wood paneling, and a brick base. The front features a covered porch, central door, and large windows.
description_view_0: Side and partial rear view showing the light wood paneling, brick base, and minimal window openings.
description_view_1: Direct front view highlighting the deep porch, supporting columns, central entrance, and large glass windows.
description_view_2: Angled front view displaying the porch, entrance door, and a set of grouped windows on the right side.
description_view_3: Rear and side view with plain wall surfaces, brick base, and no visible windows or doors.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with grouped front and side windows
